messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following telecom provider privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"ConnectTel collects your name, address, account details, call records, and usage data to provide telecommunications services. We process this under our contract with you and legal obligations as a licensed telecom operator. Call records are retained for 12 months for billing and legal compliance. With your consent, we use usage data to personalise service offers. You have the right to access, correct, erase, port, restrict, and object to your data. Contact privacy@connecttel.com or our DPO at dpo@connecttel.com. We use Nokia network infrastructure with EU data processing confirmed under a DPA. We notify the national telecoms regulator and data protection authority within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) both stated. Separate consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) for personalisation. All three bases correctly applied.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Call records and usage patterns can infer health, religious, or political information. No safeguard or acknowledgement stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Service delivery, billing, legal compliance, and personalised offers are each stated as distinct purposes with separate lawful bases.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for telecom service delivery.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 12-month call record retention stated and grounded in billing and legal compliance requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), portability (Art. 20), restriction (Art. 18), and objection (Art. 21) all listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Nokia named as network infrastructure provider with EU data processing confirmed under a DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU data processing confirmed for Nokia. No transfer concerns raised.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@connecttel.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour notification to telecoms regulator and data protection authority both stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (call record inference risk not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that call records and usage patterns may reveal sensitive information and state appropriate safeguards for such inferences
